records.ety: [L. inductus, p. p. of inducere. See Induce.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Inducted; p. pr. & vb. n. Inducting.]

defs: To bring in; to introduce; to usher in.
quotes: The independent orator inducting himself without further ceremony into the pulpit.

defs: To introduce, as to a benefice or office; to put in actual possession of the temporal rights of an ecclesiastical living, or of any other office, with the customary forms and ceremonies.
quotes: The prior, when inducted into that dignity, took an oath not to alienate any of their lands.
